defect: none. CFR source supports Apr 2022, APT-C-23 'Hamas-linked actor', targets in 'defense, law enforcement, and emergency services', Palestine sponsor, Espionage. The 'Windows and Android implants' specificity is not in the thin CFR text but is attributed to the cited Cybereason 'Operation Bearded Barbie' report; not a defect against the stated source chain.

KEY RULE: doctrine_link kp/8th-congress-defense-plan is confidence:attested to treasury jy1702, but that source never names the 8th Congress / five-year defense development plan. It only states generic WMD financing: 'This revenue provides the DPRK with resources that it uses to support its unlawful ballistic missile and nuclear weapons programs.' The reasoning itself only claims a 'standard WMD-financing attestation pattern', i.e. inference, not an explicit naming of the named strategic doctrine. Attested overreaches.

none. Microsoft source confirms 'In April 2024, Microsoft observed Moonstone Sleet delivering a new custom ransomware variant we have named FakePenny against a company it previously compromised in February... this is the first time we have observed this threat actor deploying ransomware', '$6.6M USD in BTC', Storm-1789, StarGlow Ventures, C.C. Waterfall, DeTankWar, IT-worker schemes. The Office-39 doctrine link is strongly_inferred with null attesting source, so no KEY RULE exposure.

none. KEY RULE satisfied: Treasury explicitly names the strategic-information mission - 'sanctioned cyber espionage group Kimsuky for gathering intelligence to support the DPRK's strategic objectives' and 'its cyber espionage campaigns directly support the DPRK's strategic and nuclear ambitions.' Nov 30 2023 date confirmed.

Two issues. (1) actor_id in/ntro/bitter names India's NTRO specifically, but the fetched source only says 'highly likely that TA397 is a state-backed threat actor tasked with intelligence gathering in the interests of the Indian state' - NTRO is never named (over-specification of actor). (2) Summary claims tool overlaps 'MuuyDownloader... Mysterious Elephant (APT-K-47) and Confucius' but these terms appear 0 times in the fetched part-one source (MiyaRAT/wmRAT/BDarkRAT are present). These claims rely on the unfetched part-two/Threatray report.

Multiple defects. (1) DUPLICATE doctrine_link: fr/lutte-informatique-influence appears twice, both confidence:attested, same attesting_source_id, differing only in reasoning - an internal duplicate. (2) KEY RULE: the SGDSN/VIGINUM page never names 'lutte informatique d'influence' (LII) - it describes a Russian pro-Russia propaganda network; attesting an offensive French LII doctrine as 'attested' is unsupported by the source text. (3) start_date 2022-09-01 is not grounded: source says VIGINUM analyzed 'Entre les mois de septembre et decembre 2023' and that the offensive strategy was 'mise en place a partir du mois de fevrier 2022', not September 2022.

none material. The ae/procurement-led-capability attested reasoning says 'Reuters explicitly described Karma as acquired from a non-NSO vendor' - that vendor-acquisition detail is NOT in this fetched article (idUSKCN1PO19O), which only calls Karma 'a powerful new hacking tool'; that detail is in the separate dedicated Karma article (the 390-char shell). However the fetched source extensively documents UAE procurement of capability (CyberPoint/American mercenaries/NESA), so the doctrine is broadly supported. Qatar Emir/royal targeting for ae/regional-influence-realigned IS supported ('Raven used Karma to hack an iPhone used by the Emir of Qatar... as well as the phones of close associates and his brother'), though 'GCC-blockade window' is not named. Borderline embellishment, not a refutable inflation.

KEY RULE: doctrine_link uk/ncs-2016 is confidence:attested to the CISA AA20-126A advisory, but that advisory never names the UK National Cyber Security Strategy 2016 nor any 'defend pillar'. 'National Cyber Security' appears only as the agency name 'National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC)'. The reasoning explicitly frames it as an 'operational expression of the NCS 2016 defend pillar' - an inference, not an attestation. Attested overreaches; should be downgraded.

Target SECTOR inflation. All three targets are marked sectors/civil-society (tr, cn, in), but the Securelist source identifies victim geography only, with no sector attribution: 'Our telemetry has detected victims in Turkiye, China, and India, with some systems remaining compromised for over a year.' The words 'civil society', 'dissident', 'Tibet', 'Uyghur' appear 0 times in the source. The civil-society sector is imported from actor pattern, not stated for this campaign (the doctrine reasoning itself concedes 'the doctrinal fit is inferred from actor pattern, not stated').

none. Summary's caveat that Microsoft named no specific Storm-2603 victim sectors/countries is accurate. targets and doctrine_links appropriately empty.

none. none. Targets civil-society/dissident + media/journalism supported. none. CFR source confirms Oct 2023, 'Arid Viper, a threat actor believed to operate from the Palestinian Territories, created a compromised version of the Arabic dating app Skipped', 'Target category: Civil society', 'Suspected state sponsor: Palestine, State of'. Target country=ps reflects CFR's stated sponsor; mild sponsor-vs-victim-location ambiguity is inherited from the source's own structure, not an inflation.

DUPLICATE doctrine_link: ae/digital-economy-protection appears twice, both confidence:attested, same attesting_source_id, differing only in reasoning - an internal duplicate that should be merged/deduplicated. The underlying claims are supported (source couples strategy to AI/digital economy via Al Kuwaiti at WGS and names 'protecting critical infrastructure' as a goal), so the substance is fine but the duplicated link is a data-quality defect.
